questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: What is the primary function of Dendritic Nanotubes (DNTs), recently identified in the brain?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Researchers at **Johns Hopkins University** identified a new kind of neuronal connection called **Dendritic** **Nanotubes (DNTs)**. These are tiny tubes, about 3 micrometers long and a few hundred nanometers thick, that connect dendrites of neighboring neurons.
● The team confirmed that these tubes are distinct from previously known tunnelling nanotubes (TNTs). Time-lapse imaging revealed that DNTs can form and disintegrate dynamically over minutes or hours.
● The study demonstrated that DNTs **transfer electrical signals** by allowing **charged particles** such as **calcium ions (Ca²⁺)** to move from one neuron to another. When calcium concentration in one neuron was artificially increased, nearby neurons showed a similar rise, an effect that was blocked when nanotube formation was inhibited using cytochalasin D.
● DNTs were also observed to **transport molecules**, including amyloid-beta protein, suggesting a possible link to Alzheimer’s disease progression. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, a research team has identified a different set of neuronal connections in the brain, one that might bypass synapses altogether.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It aims to achieve self-sufficiency in domestic
pulses production by 2030–31.
III. 	It provides for assured maximum procurement
of Tur, Urad, and Masoor under the Price Support Scheme.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Union Cabinet** has approved the **Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses,** a **six-year** central scheme with a financial outlay of **₹11,440 crore**.
● The mission aims to make India **self-sufficient in pulses production** and reduce dependence on imports by **2030–31**. The area under cultivation will be expanded to 310 lakh hectares from 242 lakh hectares, and the yield is expected to rise to 1,130 kg per hectare from 881 kg per hectare. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Under this mission, the government has set a target to increase pulses production to 350 lakh tonnes by 2030–31, compared to 242 lakh tonnes achieved in 2023–24.
● The mission will particularly focus on **tur (pigeon pea)**, **urad (black gram)**, and **masur (lentil)**, with **assured procurement** by government agencies such as the National Agricultural Cooperative Marketing Federation of India (NAFED) and the National Cooperative Consumers’ Federation of India Ltd (NCCF) under the **Price Support Scheme (PSS)** of the **Pradhan Mantri Annadata Aay Sanrakshan Abhiyan** **(PM-AASHA)**. **So, statement II is correct.**
● To achieve these goals, the mission will be implemented through a **cluster-based approach** across **416** **focused districts**. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Other Features of the Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses:**
● Development and commercial availability of climate-resilient seeds,
● Enhancing protein content,
● Improving post-harvest storage and management, and
● Establishment of **1,000 new processing and packaging units** with a **maximum subsidy of ₹25** **lakh** for each unit.
● States will prepare **five-year rolling seed production plans**, with **breeder seed production** **supervised by ICAR**, while **foundation and certified seed production** will be undertaken by state and central agencies and tracked through the **Seed Authentication, Traceability &** **Holistic Inventory (SATHI) portal**.
**Pulse production in India:**
● **India is the largest producer** (25% of global production), **consumer** (27% of world consumption), **and importer** (14%) of pulses **in the world.**
● **Rajasthan accounted for the most (54.67 lakh hectare) of the total 275 lakh hectare area** **under pulses** during 2023-24. It was followed by Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh.
● **In terms of pulses production, Madhya Pradesh (59.74 lakh metric tonnes) accounted for** **most** of the total production of 242 lakh metric tonnes during 2023-24, followed by Maharashtra, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh.
**Relevance:** The Union Cabinet on October 1, 2025, approved a six-year central scheme aimed at achieving selfsufficiency in pulses production.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Which of the following missions provides earth observation data useful for monitoring land deformation, biomass, and glaciers?
I. 	 ISRO PSLV- C61
II. 	 Axiom Mission
III. 	NISAR Mission
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **PSLV–C61 is not an Earth observation mission by itself;** rather, it is a launch vehicle mission operated by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation). The PSLV–C61’s primary role is to carry satellites into space, but it does not directly generate or provide Earth observation data. **So, point I is** **not correct.**
● The Axiom Mission refers to private astronaut missions conducted by Axiom Space in partnership with NASA and SpaceX. These missions send private crews to the International Space Station (ISS) to conduct scientific research, technology demonstrations, and commercial activities in microgravity. The primary objectives of Axiom Missions are human spaceflight and related experiments aboard the ISS. **So, point II is not correct.**
● NISAR (NASA–ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) is a joint Earth observation mission between NASA and ISRO. It will use dual-frequency synthetic aperture radar (L-band from NASA and S-band from ISRO) to provide high-resolution, day-and-night, all-weather imagery.
● Key objectives of the NISAR Mission: ○ Monitor land deformation (such as earthquakes, landslides, and volcanoes). ○ Measure biomass and forest changes, helping estimate the global carbon cycle. ○ Track the movement of glaciers and ice sheets, contributing to climate change studies. ○ Map agricultural patterns and coastal processes.
● The mission will orbit Earth every 12 days, providing repetitive coverage for long-term monitoring. **So,** **point III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the GSLV-F16 mission lifted off from Sriharikota on July 30, placing the NASA ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) satellite into a sun synchronous orbit.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText: The HKU5 virus is sometimes mentioned in the news with reference to which one of the following diseases?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The bat Merbecovirus HKU5 belongs to the same group as the Middle East Respiratory** **Syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV)** and shares similarities in its structure and the receptors it uses to infect cells.
● Although HKU5 has so far been detected **only in bats**, studies suggest that it has the potential to infect human cells, though no human infections have been reported yet.
● The vaccines developed for MERS-CoV have shown limited effectiveness against the HKU5 virus, highlighting the need for developing broad-spectrum vaccines capable of protecting against multiple related coronaviruses and potential future zoonotic threats. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A group of bat coronaviruses, known as HKU5 merbecoviruses, may be just one mutation away from acquiring the ability to infect humans, a new study has warned.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText: This festival is celebrated for nine days, overlapping with Navratri, and symbolizes the collective spirit of the people of Telangana. Recently, it set two Guinness World Records for the largest floral arrangement and a synchronised dance performance by women. The festival is:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Bathukamma** literally means **“The Mother Goddess comes to life”** and is dedicated to **Maha Gauri**, also known as **Gauri Devi**, the life giver and patron goddess of womanhood.
● It is celebrated for **nine days**, overlapping with Navratri, and symbolizes the **collective spirit** of the people of **Telangana**. The celebrations usually take place between **September 25 and October 3** each year. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● The festival is observed to pray for the health, prosperity, and success of families. It is especially significant for young women who seek blessings for a life partner of their choice.
●  The **Bathukamma Festival** achieved **two new Guinness World Records** in 2025 at the Saroornagar Stadium in Hyderabad. The first record was for the **largest Bathukamma floral decoration**, which consisted of about **seven tonnes of flowers** and stood 63.11 feet high and 11 feet wide. The second record was for the **largest synchronized performance** by 1,354 women, who participated in the traditional Bathukamma dance. **So, statement II is correct.**
● During the festival, women create beautiful flower arrangements using locally available exotic flowers. These flowers are stacked in a conical arrangement on a wide plate, forming the Bathukamma. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the celebrations of the Bathukamma Festival in Telangana have set two new Guinness World Records.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the recently constituted Payments Regulatory Board (PRB), consider the following statements:
I.  	 It is a six-member body chaired by the
Finance Secretary.
II. 	 It derives its powers from the Payment and
Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
III. 	It regulates all electronic and non-electronic
payment systems.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Payments Regulatory Board (PRB)** has recently been set up by the **Reserve Bank of India (RBI)** to strengthen the governance and regulatory oversight of India’s rapidly expanding payment systems. The PRB replaces the **Board for Regulation and Supervision of Payment and Settlement Systems (BPSS).**
● Th**e PRB is a six-member body,** but it is **chaired by the RBI Governor**, not the Finance Secretary. The other two RBI representatives are the **Deputy Governor** and the **Executive Director** in charge of Payment and Settlement Systems. Additionally, **three nominees** are appointed by the Central Government. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● The **Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 (PSS Act)** is the primary legal framework empowering the RBI and now the PRB to regulate and supervise payment systems in India. **So, statement II is correct.**
● One of the key functions of the PRB is the **regulation and supervision of all types of payment systems**, covering both **electronic** and **non-electronic** systems. This includes oversight of **domestic as well as** **cross-border transactions**, ensuring that India’s payment systems remain safe, efficient, and interoperable. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Payments Regulatory Board (PRB) has been recently set up by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Hazardous, Radioactive, E-Waste, Heavy Metals, etc.
questions.questionText:
Trichloroethylene (TCE), a cause of concern in recent times for its environmental and health impacts, is mainly used as:
I. 	 A chemical intermediate in the manufacturing
of refrigerants
II. 	 A degreasing solvent in metal industry
III. 	A fertilizer additive in agriculture
IV. 	 A spot remover in commercial dry-cleaning
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Trichloroethylene (TCE)** is a **volatile, colorless synthetic organic chemical** extensively used in industrial and commercial sectors. It is not naturally occurring but is produced through chemical synthesis.
● One of the **primary industrial uses of TCE** is in the **manufacture of refrigerants and other** **hydrofluorocarbons**. It acts as an essential chemical intermediate in producing compounds that are used in cooling systems such as air conditioners and refrigerators. **So, statement I is correct.**
● TCE is widely employed as a **degreasing solvent in the metal industry**. Its ability to dissolve oils, waxes, greases, and other contaminants makes it ideal for cleaning machinery, tools, and precision equipment before further processing or assembly.  **So, statement II is correct.**
● **TCE is not used as a fertilizer additive.** It is an industrial solvent and chemical intermediate with no known function as a direct component of fertilizer. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● TCE is used by **commercial dry cleaners as a spot remover**, especially for grease, oil, or wax stains on fabrics. Its strong solvent properties make it effective for cleaning applications in the textile sector. **So,** **statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recent studies have linked long-term trichloroethylene (TCE) exposure to increased risk of Parkinson’s disease, drawing global attention to its industrial regulation.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Aquatic Ecosystem and Life (Aquatic Ecosystem, Maritime Zones, Corals, Mangrove, Marine Vegetation)
questions.questionText:
The region often referred to as the Amazon of the Seas’ extends across the tropical waters of:
I. 	 Philippines
II. 	 Maldives
III. 	Malaysia
IV. 	 Papua New Guinea
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Coral Triangle** is often called the **“Amazon of the Seas”** due to its extraordinary marine biodiversity. It covers an area of about **5.7 million sq. km** in the **tropical waters of the western Pacific Ocean**, and is home to over **75% of the world’s coral species** and more than 3,000 fish species.
● The **Philippines** is one of the core countries of the Coral Triangle (Amazon of the Seas). The Philippine archipelago lies within the Indo-Pacific region, where warm tropical waters support vibrant coral reefs and diverse fish populations. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Maldives** is **not part of the Coral Triangle**. It is an island nation in the Indian Ocean, southwest of India and Sri Lanka. **So, point II is not correct.**
● **Malaysia** is one of the six countries that make up the **Coral Triangle.** It is divided into Peninsular Malaysia and East Malaysia by the South China Sea. The portion of the nation known as Peninsular Malaysia shares a border with Thailand, Singapore, Vietnam, and Indonesia while East Malaysia borders Brunei, Indonesia, Philippines, and Vietnam. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Papua New Guinea** is also a key part of the **Coral Triangle.** Its waters contain some of the most pristine and diverse coral reef systems globally. **So, point IV is correct.**
● So, the Coral Triangle is a triangular area of the tropical marine waters of Indonesia, Malaysia, Papua New Guinea, the Philippines, Solomon Islands and Timor-Leste. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Philippines is establishing coral larvae cryobank to protect threatened coral reefs by preserving their genetic diversity for future restoration.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Ig Nobel Prizes, consider the following statements:
I. 	 They are awarded by the Royal Swedish
Academy of Sciences.
II. 	 They are awarded once every five years to
promote scientific research.
III. 	They are given in several categories, including
Peace and Psychology.
IV. 	 In 2025, two Indian scientists received the
award in the Engineering Design category.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Ig Nobel Prizes** are a set of satirical awards given annually to celebrate unusual, imaginative, and humorous scientific research that “first makes people laugh, and then makes them think.” Instituted in 1991, these prizes contrast sharply with the prestigious Nobel Prizes.
● The **Ig Nobel Prizes are not awarded by the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences**. They are selected and presented by the US-based magazine **Annals of Improbable Research**. In contrast, the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences awards the Nobel Prizes in Physics, Chemistry, and Economics, while other Nobel categories are awarded by different Swedish or Norwegian institutions. The Ig Nobel ceremony takes place in Boston, not Stockholm. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● The Ig Nobel Prizes are **awarded annually**, not every five years. Moreover, their purpose is to **encourage** **public engagement with science through humour and curiosity**. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The Ig Nobels are given in a wide range of categories, often mirroring Nobel fields but with humorous or unexpected twists. For example, in 2025, awards were given in categories such as **Biology** (zebra-striped cows and flies), **Engineering Design** (shoe rack and smelly shoes), **Literature** (nail growth documentation), **Psychology**, **Chemistry**, **Nutrition**, and **Peace**. **So, statement III is correct.**
● In 2025, **Vikash Kumar and Sarthak Mittal**, two Indian scientists, received the **Ig Nobel Prize in** **Engineering Design** for their research on how foul-smelling shoes affect the experience of using a shoe rack. **So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:**  In 2025, two Indian scientists received the Ig Nobel Prize in the Engineering Design category.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Recently, there were deaths of children in Madhya
Pradesh and Rajasthan linked to contaminated cough syrup. In this context, Diethylene Glycol is mainly used in cough syrup for which one of the following reasons?

options.a: It acts as a strong sedative.
options.b: It acts as a toxic solvent.
options.c: It increases the viscosity of syrups.
options.d: It serves as a substitute of glycerin.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Diethylene glycol (DEG) is a **volatile, industrial solvent** commonly used in products like **antifreeze,** **paints, brake fluids, and plastics**. It does not occur naturally but may **contaminate pharmaceutical** **syrups**—often glycerine-based—due to poor regulatory oversight or substitution with cheaper industrialgrade materials.
● DEG is a **highly toxic solvent** when ingested. It can cause **acute kidney injury** by damaging renal tubules, leading to **renal failure**, metabolic acidosis, and multi-organ dysfunction. Children are particularly vulnerable, and exposure through contaminated cough syrups has led to **fatalities globally**. WHO estimates around **300 child deaths worldwide** from DEG contamination since 2022.
● Since 2022, the **WHO has issued multiple global medical alerts** after DEG-contaminated syrups were linked to child fatalities in various countries, including The Gambia, India, and parts of Southeast Asia. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recent deaths of children in Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan are linked to contaminated cough syrup containing Diethylene glycol.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.questionText:
International Social Security Association (ISSA),
works under which of the following organizations?

options.b:
Organisation for Economic Co-operation and
Development (OECD)

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **International Social Security Association (ISSA)** is the world’s leading organization for social security institutions, government agencies, and departments. It works to promote **excellence in the**
**administration of social security** worldwide through professional guidelines, research, and capacitybuilding programs.
● The ISSA was **founded in 1927** under the auspices of the **International Labour Organization (ILO).** **So, statement I is correct.**
● The International Social Security Association (ISSA) has **over 330 member organizations** from 158 countries, and **India is a member country. So, statement II is correct.**
● The **headquarters** of the ISSA are located in **Geneva, Switzerland,** which **also hosts the ILO** and other major international bodies related to labour and human welfare. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India has been conferred the prestigious International Social Security Association Award 2025 for outstanding achievement in providing social security.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Fighter Jet Developed by
I. - JF-17 Thunder USA
II. - Mirage 2000 France
III. - F-22 Raptor United Kingdom
IV. - Shenyang J-35 Japan
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **JF-17 Thunder** is a **jointly developed**, **single-engine**, **multi-role fighter jet** built by **China** and **Pakistan**. It is a modern, **4.5-generation aircraft** powered by a Russian-designed turbofan engine and equipped with a range of Chinese and other multinational components, including **modern avionics and** **advanced weaponry.** The JF-17 serves as the backbone of the Pakistan Air Force. **So, pair I is not** **correctly matched.**
● The **Mirage 2000** is a **French-made,** single-engine, **delta-wing fighter** jet, known for its multirole capabilities, maneuverability, and speed. It features a powerful afterburning turbofan engine, two 30mm cannons, and multiple hardpoints. The **Indian Air Force** uses upgraded Mirage 2000s and they have a significant combat history, including operations in the **Kargil War and the 2019 Balakot airstrike.** **So,** **pair II is correctly matched.**
● The **F-22 Raptor** is an **American** twin-engine, **fifth-generation stealth** fighter aircraft, first flown in 1997 and entering service in 2005. It combines **stealth, speed, agility,** and advanced avionics to achieve air dominance. Due to its cost and evolving threats, production of the F-22 **ended in 2011.** **So, pair III is** **not correctly matched.**
● The **Shenyang J-35** is a **Chinese twin-engine,** twin-seat, stealth multirole combat aircraft designed for air superiority and **surface strike missions,** intended for both carrier and land-based operations. It is a significant leap in China's airpower and a **potential rival to the US F-35.** **So, pair IV is not correctly** **matched.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Russian defence experts recently stated that the reported sale of RD-93 engines by Russia to Pakistan for integration into its JF-17 fighter jets could actually benefit India.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part III, IV & IVA: Rights and Duties
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Preventive Detention in India:
I. 	 The Constitutional protection against preventive detention extends to both citizens and non-citizens.
II. 	 A person detained under preventive detention
must be informed of the grounds for such detention as a matter of right.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Preventive detention** refers to the **confinement** of a person **without trial,** not as punishment for a past act but to **prevent possible future offenses.** It is a precautionary measure, provided under **Article 22** of the Indian Constitution, applicable even in peacetime.
● Article 22 of the Indian Constitution provides protection from and against arrest and detention in certain cases. **These safeguards are available to all people, irrespective of whether they are citizens of India**
**or not.** However, Article 22(3) states that the said clauses are not applicable to: an enemy alien; or any person who has been arrested or detained under any preventive detention law. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Under **Article 22(5),** it is mandatory to **communicate to the detenu the grounds for detention** so that they may make a representation against it. However, the authority may **withhold** facts if disclosure is deemed against **the public interest. So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Sonam Wangchuk’s preventive detention under the National Security Act (NSA), 1980 renewed debate on constitutional safeguards in preventive detention laws.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Physiography of India (Location, Division, Physiographic Regions)
questions.questionText:
The highest motorable road in the world was
recently built in Ladakh at which one of the following passes?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Border Roads Organisation (BRO)** recently achieved a historic milestone by constructing the **world’s highest motorable road** at **Mig La Pass**, situated 19,400 feet above sea level in **Ladakh**.
● This feat, accomplished under **Project Himank** and led by Brig Vishal Srivastava, broke BRO’s own previous record of 19,024 feet at **Umling La, set in 2021.**
● The new **Likaru–Mig La–Fukche road** holds immense strategic importance, as it provides a third vital axis to Fukche village, near the **Line of Actual Control (LAC).**
● It will also **enhance tourism** by offering spectacular views of the Indus Valley and improving **accessibility** to remote border regions.
● **Khardung La Pass:** It is positioned on the Ladakh range and is the gateway to Shyok and Nubra valleys.
● **Umling La Pass:** It is located in Eastern Ladakh, connecting Chisumle and Demchok near the Indo-China border.
● Chang La Pass: It is also located in Ladakh. It is named after the Sadhu Changla Baba. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About Project Himank:**
● It is a flagship initiative of the **Border Roads Organisation (BRO)**, responsible for developing and maintaining **roads** in the **high-altitude areas of Ladakh.**
● Established in 1985, the project operates under BRO’s Project Himank Division **(also** **known as the “Mountain Tamers”),** headquartered at Leh.
● It has executed several **record-setting infrastructure projects**, including roads over Khardung La, Tanglang La, and Umling La, among the world’s highest passes.
**Relevance:** The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) recently achieved a historic milestone by constructing the world’s highest motorable road at Mig La Pass.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
The term ‘Supermoon’, sometimes mentioned in
the news, refers to which one of the following?

options.a:
The alignment of the Sun, Earth, and Moon
causing a total solar eclipse
options.b:
A full moon that occurs when the Moon is at
its farthest point from Earth
options.c:
A full or new moon that coincides with the
Moon’s closest approach to Earth
options.d:
A lunar event visible only during equinoxes
when the Moon crosses the Earth’s equatorial plane

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **Supermoon** occurs when a **full moon or new moon** coincides with the Moon’s closest approach to Earth **(perigee)** in its **elliptical orbit.**
● Since the Moon’s orbit is not a perfect circle, its distance from Earth varies by about **50,000 km every** **month**. When the Moon reaches perigee and aligns opposite the Sun, it appears up to **14% larger and** **about 30% brighter** than at apogee (its farthest point). Though the size difference is subtle to the naked eye, its **enhanced luminosity makes it visually striking.**
● Supermoons also strengthen **gravitational interactions,** causing perigean spring tides that slightly increase **coastal water levels.** Beyond the scientific appeal, these events hold **cultural**, photographic, and **astronomical significance**, inspiring **folklore** and offering clearer observation of lunar features. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** A majestic Harvest Supermoon was observed on October 6–7, 2025, appearing exceptionally bright and large over the eastern horizon with Saturn visible nearby in the constellation Pisces, captivating skywatchers around the world.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Biodiversity (IUCN, WWF, CBD, Cartagena, Nagoya, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Marine Stewardship Council (MSC):
I. 	 It sets global standards for sustainable fishing
and seafood traceability.
II. 	 Its certification applies to aquaculture
fisheries as well as wild-capture fisheries.
III. 	The Ashtamudi clam fishery in Kerela was
the first in India to be awarded the MSC
certification.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Marine Stewardship Council (MSC)** is an **international non-profit** organisation that promotes sustainable fishing by setting **globally recognised, science-based standards for sustainable fishing** and **seafood traceability.** Its **blue ecolabel** assures consumers that seafood comes from environmentally responsible and well-managed fisheries. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **MSC certification is voluntary and is available to all wild-capture fisheries,** regardless of their size, scale, ecology, geography or technology. Small-scale and **community-based fisheries** can equally apply if they meet the **MSC’s three principles:** stock health, ecosystem impact, and effective management. The **Aquaculture Stewardship Council (ASC) is the certification body for aquaculture fisheries. So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● The MSC program uniquely aligns with the **FAO’s internationally recognised frameworks** for sustainable fishing and eco-labelling. It remains the **only certification scheme for wild-capture fisheries** that explicitly incorporates these FAO codes, ensuring credibility and global comparability of sustainability standards.
● The **Astamudi clam (Paphia malabarica) fishery in Kerala** became **India’s first to earn MSC** **certification**, recognising its sustainable management of wild clam resources. This milestone highlights **community-based conservation** and responsible fishing practices in India’s coastal ecosystems, promoting eco-friendly livelihood opportunities. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** About 10 Indian marine and saline fish and shrimp varieties are set to get the global Marine Stewardship Council (MSC) certification soon.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the recently launched Foreign
Currency Settlement System, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It was established under the Payment and
Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
II. 	 It is operated by the Reserve Bank of India as
part of its global settlement framework.
III. 	It supports settlements only in US dollar,
reflecting its dominant role in global trade
and finance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Foreign Currency Settlement System (FCSS)** is a pioneering initiative aimed at strengthening India’s position as a **global financial hub** through GIFT IFSC (International Financial Services Centre). It enables local settlement of foreign currency transactions between **IFSC Banking Units (IBUs),** eliminating the dependence on foreign correspondent banking routes and significantly reducing settlement time from up to 48 hours to near real-time.
● The FCSS has been **established** under the comprehensive legal and regulatory framework of the **Payment** **and Settlement Systems Act, 2007,** which governs all payment and settlement mechanisms in India. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● The system is **authorised** by **International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA).**  It is **operated by the CCIL IFSC Limited (CCIL IFSC),** a subsidiary of Clearing Corporation of India
Limited. Currently, foreign currency transactions in GIFT IFSC are processed via **correspondent** **banking routes** i.e through multiple Nostro account relationships (accounts held with foreign banks) and intermediaries to route funds. That chain of relay can lead to **settlement lags of 36 to 48 hours. So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● **The FCSS is meant for foreign currency settlements within GIFT IFSC, not domestic rupee** **settlements. Initially**, the FCSS supported **US dollar settlements**, reflecting its dominant role in global trade and finance. However, the framework is designed with flexibility, allowing the **future inclusion of** **other major currencies** like the euro, pound sterling, or yen as the system scales. **So, statement III is** **not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About the International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA)**
● It is a statutory authority established under **the International Financial Services Centres** **Authority Act, 2019.**
● It is a **unified authority** for the development and regulation of **financial products, financial** **services, and financial institutions** in the International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) in India.
● **Headquarter:** GIFT City, Gandhinagar, in Gujarat
**Relevance:** Recently, the union Finance Minister launched a Foreign Currency Settlement System (FCSS) at the International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) in GIFT City.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
A startup valued at over $1 billion and not listed
on the stock exchange is known as a:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A unicorn is a **privately owned startup company** valued at **over $1 billion.** The term was popularized by **venture capitalist Aileen Lee in 2013** to describe such rare and successful startups.
● **Key characteristics of unicorns include** private ownership, high growth potential, innovative or **disruptive technology,** and the ability to attract significant investment from venture capitalists. Their valuations are largely based on **future growth potential, not just current profits.**
● **Unicorns aren't publicly traded companies** and most investors must wait until their shares are made available to the public through an IPO to invest in them, remain private, or be acquired by larger corporations. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Key Startup Ecosystem Terms**
● **Soonicorn:** A Soonicorn is a startup that is on the verge of becoming a unicorn, typically valued between $500 million and $1 billion. These companies show rapid growth and strong investor interest.
● **Decacorn:** A Decacorn refers to a privately held startup valued at over $10 billion. Such companies demonstrate exceptional market dominance, global reach, and consistent high growth in their industries.
● **Hectocorn:** A Hectocorn is a company valued at over $100 billion. These are rare, highly successful firms like Google or Apple, symbolizing long-term stability, innovation, and massive global impact.
● **Gazelle:** A Gazelle is a startup founded after 2000 with the potential to become a unicorn within two years. Their valuations generally range from $500 million to $1 billion, showcasing fast growth.
● **Cheetah:** A Cheetah is a startup expected to achieve unicorn status within four years. Typically valued between $200 million and $500 million, cheetahs exhibit strong innovation and early-stage expansion potential.
**Relevance:** India has solidified its position as the world’s third-largest startup ecosystem, boasting 73 unicorns in 2025, up from just one in 2011 according to the Hurun Global Unicorn Index 2025.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja
Suraksha evam Utthan Mahabhiyan Scheme (PMKUSUM), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It aims to provide free or subsidised electricity
to farmers through the installation of rooftop solar systems.
II. 	 It targets to add solar capacity of about 34,800
MW by March 2026.
III. 	Its beneficiaries include only individual
farmers and not any group of farmers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **PM-KUSUM Scheme**, launched in 2019 by the **Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE),** aims to provide **energy and water security** to farmers while **promoting renewable energy** and reducing dependence on diesel for irrigation. It seeks to enhance farmers’ income through decentralized solar power generation and sale of **surplus power to DISCOMs.**
● The **objective** of providing **free or low-cost electricity** through rooftop solar pertains to the **PM–Surya** **Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana,** not PM-KUSUM. The PM-KUSUM scheme is designed to **solarize** **agricultural pumps** and encourage decentralized solar generation in the farm sector. **So, statement I is** **not correct.**
● The scheme has a target to add about **34,800 MW of solar capacity by March 2026** through its three components — decentralized solar plants, standalone solar pumps, and solarization of grid-connected pumps. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Urban residential households are not eligible** beneficiaries under the PM-KUSUM Scheme. The beneficiaries include **individual farmers, groups of farmers, FPOs, Panchayats, Co-operatives, and** **Water User Associations**, focusing exclusively on the agricultural and rural sector. **So, statement III is** **not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About the International Solar Alliance**
● It was launched in **2015** by **India and France** during the **UNFCCC’s COP 21 in Paris**.
● **Key objectives:** ○ To deploy **1,000 GW** of solar energy capacity globally **by 2030**. ○ To mobilize more than **US$1 trillion in investment** for solar energy deployment in member countries **by 2030.** ○ To develop **innovative financial mechanisms** to reduce the cost of solar energy deployment.
● **Membership:** Total **120 member and signatory countries.** ○ All member states of the **United Nations** are **eligible** to join the ISA.
● **Headquarters:** **Gurugram, India** (first international organization established in the country).
**Relevance:** The Union government is looking to showcase the PM-KUSUM programme to several African countries and island nations by using the International Solar Alliance platform.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Union Public Service
Commission (UPSC), consider the following statements:
I. 	 Part XX of the Constitution deals with the
composition and functions of the UPSC.
II. 	 It was set up based on the recommendations
of the Lee Commission (1924).
III. 	Its first Indian Chairman after Independence
was H. K. Kripalani.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **UPSC** is an **independent Constitutional** body established under **Article 315 of the Constitution**, not Article 314. **Article 315 to 323** in Part XIV of the Constitution deals with the composition, appointment, and functions of the UPSC and State Public Service Commissions. **So, statement I is not** **correct.**
● The UPSC was set up based on the recommendations of the **Lee Commission (1924).** Following the commission's recommendations, the first Public Service Commission was established on **October 1, 1926.** The commission was later renamed the **Federal Public Service Commission** under the **Government of** **India Act of 1935.** With the adoption of the Constitution of India on January 26, 1950, it became the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC). **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **first Indian Chairman** of the UPSC **after independence** was **H.K. Kripalani**, who served from **April 1, 1947, to January 13, 1949.** He assumed the role after the country's independence and was initially the chairman of the Federal Public Service Commission, which was renamed the Union Public Service Commission. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) marked its centenary year from 1 October 2025 to 1 October 2026, celebrating 100 years of transparent and merit-based recruitment for India’s civil services.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Which of the following features distinguish Tejas
Mk1A from the earlier Tejas Mk1 version?
I. 	 4.5 generation aircraft
II. 	 Advanced electronically scanned array radar
III. 	Delta wing design
IV. 	 Integration with Astra missile system
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Tejas Light Combat Aircraft (LCA)** programme, developed by **Hindustan Aeronautics Limited** **(HAL)** and **DRDO**, is India’s first indigenous multirole fighter initiative.
● The **Tejas Mk1A** is classified as a **4.5-generation fighter** because it integrates systems such as AESA radar, an Electronic Warfare (EW) suite, digital fly-by-wire controls, and network-centric warfare features.  The **earlier Tejas Mk1** is considered a **4th-generation** aircraft. **So, point I is correct.**
● **The original Tejas Mk1 fighter jet does not have an Advanced Electronically Scanned Array** **(AESA)** radar, but the **newer Tejas Mk1A  is equipped with the Israeli EL/M-2052 AESA radar** in its initial production run, with plans to replace it with the indigenous Uttam AESA radar in later batches. **So,** **point II is correct.**
● **Delta Wing Design:** The tailless delta wing design is a **common feature in both Mk1 and Mk1A** **variants.** It provides aerodynamic stability and agility, but does not differentiate Mk1A from its predecessor. **So, point III is not correct.**
● **Integration with Indigenous Astra Missile:** Mk1A is compatible with the indigenous **Astra Beyond** **Visual Range (BVR)** air-to-air missile, developed by DRDO. Mk1 was limited to imported BVR missiles like **Derby and R-77. So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The indigenous Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas Mk 1A undertook its first public sortie at the Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) facility in Nashik recently.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Crew Escape System (CES),
consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is an emergency escape system that pulls
the crew module with astronauts to a safe distance in case of launch failure.
II. 	 India’s Gaganyaan mission will employ a
Puller-type Crew Escape System.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Crew Escape System (CES)** is an **emergency safety mechanism** designed to ensure astronaut survival during a launch emergency. It **separates the crew module** from a **malfunctioning launch** **vehicle** and propels it to a safe distance, after which a multistage parachute system slows its descent for a **safe splashdown.** The CES plays a vital role in human spaceflight missions. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The CES is **most crucial during the initial atmospheric phase of flight**, when aerodynamic pressure and dynamic loads are highest. Once the vehicle reaches orbit insertion, aborts are handled by **onboard** **propulsion** systems instead.
● The CES is of **two types**. **The Puller type** uses **solid motors** to pull the module away, while the **Pusher** type uses **liquid engines** to push it. India’s **Gaganyaan mission** employs a **Puller-type CES**, similar to those used in the **Saturn V** (U.S.), **Soyuz** (Russia), and **Long March** (China) missions. This design ensures quick and reliable separation during an emergency. **Pusher-type** is used in **SpaceX Crew Dragon** **(Falcon 9)**. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** ISRO has developed a cost-effective, single-stage test vehicle powered by the Vikas engine to validate the crew escape system (CES).

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Mission Drishti, recently seen in the news, is
related to:

options.a:
India’s first interplanetary communication
satellite for Mars missions
options.b:
World’s first multi-sensor Earth Observation
(EO) satellite
options.c:
India’s first underground neutrino observatory
for studying neutrinos
options.d:
A deep-space telescope mission jointly
developed by ISRO and NASA

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Mission Drishti** is the **world’s first multi-sensor Earth Observation (EO) satellite** developed by **Bengaluru-based space startup GalaxEye.**
● It is **India’s largest privately built satellite** and the country’s highest-resolution satellite, offering 1.5metre resolution.
● The 160 kg satellite carries both **Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)** and high-resolution optical payloads, allowing imaging in all weather and at any time of day. It has passed rigorous structural tests at **ISRO’s** **U R Rao Satellite Centre.**
● GalaxEye plans to deploy 8–12 satellites by 2029. Mission Drishti supports diverse applications such as **border surveillance, disaster management, defence, infrastructure monitoring, agriculture**, and financial assessments through advanced geospatial analytics. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Space-tech start-up GalaxEye will launch the world’s first multi-sensor Earth observation (EO) satellite ‘Mission Drishti’ in the first quarter of next year.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Nobel Peace Prize:
I. 	 Self-nomination leads to automatic disqualification.
II. 	 Nominations for the Nobel Peace Prize remain
confidential for 50 years after submission.
III. 	In 2025, it was awarded to Maria Corina
Machado for her efforts to promote democracy in Venezuela.
IV. 	 It is the only one which is awarded in Sweden
among all Nobel Prizes.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Nobel Prizes** were established under the will of **Alfred Nobel**, a Swedish inventor, chemist, and philanthropist, in **1895.** They have been **awarded annually** since 1901 for outstanding contributions in **Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, Literature, and Peace.** Later, in 1968, Sweden’s central bank—Sveriges Riksbank—instituted the **Economic Sciences** Prize in Nobel’s memory.
● **No person can nominate herself/himself for a Nobel Prize.** Nominators are required to submit a detailed written proposal highlighting the candidate's achievements, while **self-nomination leads to automatic** **disqualification. So, statement I is correct.**
● **Nominations for all Nobel Prizes,** including the Peace Prize, are kept strictly **confidential for 50 years** to protect the privacy of nominees and deliberators. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Norwegian Nobel Committee,** a five-member body appointed by the **Norwegian Parliament** **(Storting)**, **selects the Nobel Peace Prize** winner each year, **not a cabinet committee.**
● In **2025, the Nobel Peace Prize** was awarded to **María Corina Machado** for her **non-violent struggle** **for democracy** and human rights in **Venezuela**, recognizing her efforts toward a peaceful political transition. **So, statement III is correct.**
● **All Nobel Prizes are awarded in Stockholm, Sweden**, except for the **Nobel Peace Prize**, which is awarded in **Oslo, Norway.** The prizes in Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, and Literature are presented in Stockholm, while the Peace Prize is awarded in Oslo as stipulated in **Alfred Nobel's will.** **So, statement IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**2025 Nobel Prize Winners**
**Category** **Winners** **Award Given For**
Physiology or Medicine
Mary E. Brunkow, Fred Ramsdell and Shimon Sakaguchi
For their discoveries concerning peripheral immune tolerance.
Physics John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret and John M. Martinis
For the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit.
Chemistry Susumu Kitagawa, Richard Robson and Omar M. Yaghi
For the development of metal– organic frameworks
Literature László Krasznahorkai For his compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
Peace Maria Corina Machado For her tireless work promoting democratic rights for the people of Venezuela and for her struggle to achieve a just and peaceful transition from dictatorship to democracy
Economic Sciences
Joel Mokyr, Philippe Aghion and Peter Howitt
For having explained innovationdriven economic growth
**Relevance:** Nobel Peace Prize 2025 awarded to Maria Corina Machado for promoting democracy in Venezuela.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
The Global Antibiotic Resistance Surveillance
Report is published by:

options.c:
Global Antibiotic Research and Development
Partnership (GARDP)

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Global Antibiotic Resistance Surveillance Report 2025**, released by the **World Health** **Organization (WHO)** under its **Global Antimicrobial Resistance and Use Surveillance System** **(GLASS)**, provides a comprehensive **global analysis of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) trends.**
● The report is based on data from over 23 million confirmed cases of bloodstream, urinary tract, gastrointestinal, and urogenital infections reported by **104 countries in 2023 and 110 countries between** **2016–2023.**
● It presents adjusted global, regional, and national estimates of AMR across 93 infection–pathogen– antibiotic combinations and **tracks resistance trends for 16 key combinations.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About the Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)**
● AMR occurs when microorganisms such as **bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites** evolve to **resist the effects of antimicrobial drugs,** rendering treatments ineffective.
● This resistance develops mainly due to **overuse and misuse of antibiotics** in humans, animals, and agriculture, along with poor infection control and lack of new drug development.
● AMR causes about **1.27 million deaths annually worldwide,** according to WHO estimates.
● It **threatens modern medicine,** making common infections harder to treat and increasing healthcare costs globally.
**Relevance:** The World Health Organization (WHO) has recently released the Global Antibiotic Resistance Surveillance Report 2025.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Hydropower Project State
I. - Upper Subansiri Arunachal Pradesh
II. - Kiru Sikkim
III. Naying Assam
IV. - Etalin Arunachal Pradesh
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Upper Subansiri Hydroelectric Project** is a **1,800 MW** run-of-the-river hydropower project planned on the Subansiri River in **Arunachal Pradesh**, India, with an expected commercial operation date in 2031. It is being developed by the **National Hydroelectric Power Corporation (NHPC)** and is a subject of recent local protests regarding its potential environmental and social impacts. The project is expected to generate approximately 6,581.29 GWh of electricity annually. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● The **Kiru Hydroelectric Power Project** is a run-of-the-river scheme located on the **Chenab River** in **Jammu and Kashmir.** It is positioned between the Kirthai-II project (upstream) and the Kwar project (downstream). The project is being implemented by **Chenab Valley Power Projects (CVPP),** a joint venture comprising the NHPC and the Jammu & Kashmir State Power Development Corporation (JKSPDC), each holding 49%, along with the Power Trading Corporation (PTC) holding the remaining 2% stake. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● The **Naying Hydroelectric Project** is a proposed **1,000 MW** hydropower project to be developed on the Siyom (Yomgo) River in **Arunachal Pradesh.** The project will be executed by the **North Eastern** **Electric Power Corporation (NEEPCO)** and will involve the construction of a concrete dam, underground powerhouse and tunnel system. Its construction is expected to commence around 2028, with commissioning targeted for 2032. Once operational, the project is projected to generate approximately **4,966.77 GWh of electricity per year. So, pair III is not correctly matched.**
● The Environment Ministry’s Forest Advisory Committee (FAC) has recently granted in-principle forest clearance for the **3,097 MW Etalin Hydroelectric Project in Arunachal Pradesh’s Dibang Valley.** The project entails the diversion of 1,175 hectares of forest land and the felling of approximately 2.78 lakh
trees and has been the subject of sustained environmental protests. It ranks among India s largest proposed hydropower projects in terms of installed capacity. **So, pair IV is correctly matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Environment Ministry’s Forest Advisory Committee (FAC) has recently granted forest clearance for the Etalin Hydroelectric Project in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Aquatic Ecosystem and Life (Aquatic Ecosystem, Maritime Zones, Corals, Mangrove, Marine Vegetation)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
International Blue Flag Certification:
I. 	 It is awarded only to beaches located within
marine protected areas.
II. It is accorded by the United Nations
Environment Programme (UNEP).
III. 	Shrivardhan and Nagaon beaches in Maharashtra received the certification in 2025.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Blue Flag Certification** is an internationally recognised eco-label awarded by the **Foundation for** **Environmental Education (FEE)**, based in Denmark. It is conferred on **beaches, marinas and** **sustainable tourism boats** that satisfy 33 stringent criteria covering cleanliness, beauty and environmental sustainability. It is **not awarded only to beaches within marine protected areas. So,** **statements I and II are not correct.**
● It was launched in France in 1985 and extended to regions outside Europe in 2001. The programme is among the world’s most prestigious voluntary environmental awards. It aims to foster sustainable development in freshwater and marine ecosystems through four key parameters — water quality, environmental management, environmental education and safety.
● In **October 2025**, five beaches in **Maharashtra** received the international Blue Flag certification. The list includes **Shrivardhan and Nagaon beaches** in Raigad district, Parnaka in Palghar and Guhagar and Ladghar beaches in Ratnagiri district. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, five Maharashtra beaches received the prestigious International Blue Flag Certification.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Pradhan Mantri Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY):
I. 	 It consolidates existing agricultural schemes
including PM-KISAN and Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana.
II. 	 It aims to transform 100 low-performing
agricultural districts across India.
III. 	At local level, it is operated by the District
Dhan Dhaanya Samitis led by District Collectors.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Union Cabinet approved the **Prime Minister Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY),** aimed at developing **100 agricultural districts** by integrating 36 schemes across 11 ministries.
● **PMDDKY consolidates 36 existing agricultural schemes** across 11 ministries, including PM-KISAN (cash transfers), Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana  (crop insurance), Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (irrigation), and Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana (RKVY), into a unified program to streamline efforts and maximise impact. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The PMDDKY has been designed on the lines of the **Aspirational Districts Programme (ADP),** launched in 2018. It targets 100 underperforming districts where farming faces challenges like low crop yields, water scarcity, and limited access to resources. Every district will have central and state agriculture universities as technical knowledge partners. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The scheme operates under the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare, with oversight from a National Steering Committee, state-level nodal committees, and District Dhan Dhaanya Samitis led by district collectors. PMDDKY districts will be ranked based on their performance. The progress of the scheme in each Dhan-Dhaanya district will be monitored monthly on 117 Key Performance Indicators, the statement said. A portal/ dashboard will be developed for this purpose. **So, statement III is correct.**
● The scheme carries an annual outlay of ₹24,000 crore and will be implemented over six years, **starting** **from Rabi season of 2025-26 and continuing till 2030-31.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Union Cabinet has approved the Prime Minister Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (PMDDKY).

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Physiography of India (Location, Division, Physiographic Regions)
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following separates the Rann of
Kutch in Gujarat from Pakistan’s Sindh Province?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Sir Creek,** originally called **‘Ban Ganga’,** is a **96-** **km estuary** that flows into the **Arabian Sea,** marking the boundary between the **Rann of Kutch** **in Gujarat and Pakistan’s Sindh Province**. It was renamed after a British official during the colonial period. Both India and Pakistan claim rights over their Territorial Sea, Exclusive Economic Zone and Continental Shelf.
● During the British Raj, the **Rann of Kutch** was part of the **Princely State of Kutch,** whereas **Sind** was administered directly under the **Bombay** **Presidency.** In 1908, the Kutch Darbar and the Sind administration laid conflicting claims over a small area in the western Rann. The dispute was eventually resolved by the British authorities when the Bombay government passed a resolution on **24 February** **1914**, officially delineating the borders between the two regions.
● Pakistan claims that the borders between Kutch and Sindh lies on the eastern bank of Sir Creek i.e. the green line connecting the mouth and top of Sir Creek as mentioned in Paragraph 9 of the resolution. However, India claims that that Paragraph 10 clarifies that the adopted line extends from the top of Sir Creek, east to where it joins Sindh — depicted in blue dotted lines. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India has expressed concern over Pakistan’s ongoing militarisation along Sir Creek, the estuary in the Rann of Kutch.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Disease Description
I. - False Smut Fungal disease in rice
II. - Leptospirosis Bacterial infection found in contaminated water
III. Crohn's disease Chronic inflammatory bowel disease
IV. Keratoconus Progressive neurodegenerative disorder
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **False smut** is a **fungal disease in rice** caused by **Ustilaginoidea virens,** which transforms individual rice grains into yellowish, velvety, egg-shaped balls that later turn greenish-black. It is most severe in **warm,** **humid, and rainy conditions** and is favoured by high nitrogen content in soil. This disease can cause significant yield loss and produce mycotoxins that are harmful to humans and animals. **So, pair I is** **correctly matched.**
● **Leptospirosis** is a disease caused by the **bacterium Leptospira** that can be found in **contaminated water** **or soil.** It is transmitted from animals to humans through contact with their urine or contaminated soil and water. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Crohn's disease** is a chronic **inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)** that causes inflammation in the digestive tract, leading to symptoms like abdominal pain, diarrhea, weight loss, fatigue and malnutrition. It can affect any part of the GI tract from the mouth to the anus, though it most often affects the small and large intestines. **So, pair III is correctly matched.**
● **Keratoconus** is a progressive eye condition where the **cornea thins and bulges into a cone shape,** **causing blurred or distorted vision**, sensitivity to light, and glare. It typically begins in adolescence or early adulthood and can worsen over time, affecting both eyes.
○ **Parkinson's disease** is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that **primarily affects** **movement due to a loss of dopamine-producing brain cells,** leading to symptoms like tremors, muscle stiffness, slowness of movement, and balance problems. **So, pair IV is not correctly** **matched.** **So, only three of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** According to recent reports, the paddy crop has been infested with false smut disease, causing widespread damage across Punjab.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Scheme Objective
I. - PRIP Scheme Provides 100% funding for all private startups in pharma sector
II. - PM-SETU Scheme
Provides financial support to students for upgrading skills
III. PM
Vishwakarma Scheme
Supports artisans with credit and toolkit
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Scheme for Promotion of Research and Innovation in the Pharma MedTech sector (PRIP)** has been launched by the **Department of Pharmaceuticals**, **Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers**, with the goal of transforming **India into a global powerhouse for R&D** in the **Pharma MedTech sector**.
○ The scale of financial assistance for early stage projects is 100% for costs up to ₹1 crore and 50% of additional cost beyond ₹1 crore, subject to a maximum up to ₹5 crore. The scale for financial assistance for later stage projects is 35% of project cost, subject to a maximum of ₹100 crore. **So,** **pair I is not correctly matched.** ○ The scheme **enables rapid discovery, development, and commercialization of drugs**.
● **PM-SETU (Pradhan Mantri Skilling and Employability Transformation through Upgraded ITIs)** is a **₹60,000 crore scheme** launched by **Prime Minister Narendra Modi** in **October 2025** to modernize **1,000 government Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs)** across India. It does not provide direct financial support or allowances to individual students. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
○ The scheme aims to **transform ITIs into modern, industry-aligned training institutions** offering **demand-driven and updated courses** relevant to the changing job market. ○ It will operate on a **hub-and-spoke model**, comprising **200 hub ITIs** and **800 spoke ITIs** to ensure widespread access and skill standardization.
● **PM Vishwakarma** is a **Central Sector Scheme** launched on **17th September 2023** to support **artisans** **and craftspeople** working with their **hands and traditional tools**. It covers **18 traditional trades**.
○ It provides a **toolkit worth up to ₹15,000 i**n the form of **e-vouchers** during Basic Skill Training. ○ Artisans receive a PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card acknowledging their craft. It offers Basic Training (5–7 days) and Advanced Training (15 days or more) with a **stipend of ₹500 per** **day.** ○ It also offers **collateral-free Enterprise Development Loans up to ₹3 lakh** in two tranches — ₹1 lakh and ₹2 lakh — with interest at 5%, and 8% government interest subvention. **So, pair III** **is correctly matched.**
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the government has invited proposals for the Rs 5,000 crore Promotion of Research and Innovation in Pharma-MedTech (PRIP) scheme.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
With reference to recent military exercises
involving India, consider the following pairs:
Exercise Country
I. - Drone Kavach United States
II. - Passage Exercise (PASSEX)
Russia
III. KONKAN-25 United Kingdom
IV. Bold Kurukshetra Singapore
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Indian Army’s Spear Corps**, under the **Eastern Command (not Indian Air Force and United** **States Air Force)**, conducted a **‘Drone Kavach’ exercise** in **eastern Arunachal Pradesh** from **September 25–28, 2025**. **Pair I is not correctly matched.**
○ The exercise aimed to **enhance combat readiness** and validate the Army s preparedness for **next-** **generation drone warfare** in forward operational zones.
● The **Indian Navy** and the **United Kingdom’s Royal Navy** conducted a **joint Passage Exercise** **(PASSEX)** in the North Arabian Sea on June 9–10, 2025, to strengthen operational interoperability and maritime partnership. **Pair II is not correctly matched.**
○ The Indian Navy was represented by the stealth frigate INS Tabar, a submarine, and a P-8I maritime patrol aircraft, while the U.K. Carrier Strike Group included the aircraft carrier HMS Prince of Wales and the frigate HMS Richmond.
● The sea phase of **Exercise KONKAN 25**, a **bilateral maritime exercise** between the **Indian Navy** and the **Royal Navy of the United Kingdom**, concluded on October 8, 2025, in the Arabian Sea. **Pair III is** **correctly matched.**
● The 14th edition of the **India–Singapore Joint Military Exercise ‘Bold Kurukshetra 2025’** commenced in **Jodhpur** and will continue till the **4th of next month**. **Pair IV is correctly matched.**
○ The exercise is being conducted as a tabletop exercise and computer-based wargame, designed to validate operational procedures for mechanised warfare.
● The 4th edition of the **India–Australia joint military exercise AUSTRAHIND-2025** began on October 13, 2025, at Irwin Barracks, Perth, Australia, and continued till October 26, 2025. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Spear Corps under Eastern Command of the Indian Army conducted Exercise 'Drone Kavach' in the forward areas of Eastern Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Wildlife Conservation (Tiger, Elephant, Lion, Rhino, Crocodile, Vulture, Tortoise, Dolphin, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Green Sea Turtle, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 It is categorised as endangered in the IUCN
Red List.
II. 	 It is a keystone marine species found in
tropical and subtropical waters.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Green Sea Turtle (Chelonia mydas)** is a keystone marine species found in tropical and subtropical waters worldwide.
● Its population has **increased by about 28% since the 1970s**, according to the IUCN.
● Its **IUCN Red List status** has been improved from **Endangered to Least Concern** due to decades of sustained conservation efforts. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● Green turtles are considered a keystone species, which means they play an important role in marine ecosystems and help maintain the ocean's ecological balance. They are found in **tropical and subtropical** **waters** around the globe, maintaining the health of coral reefs and seagrass meadows. **So, statement II is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Red List status of the green sea turtle (Chelonia mydas) has improved from Endangered to Least Concern due to sustained conservation.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following rivers forms part of
the international boundary between India and Bangladesh?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
A Trans-boundary river is a river that crosses at least one political border, either a border within a nation or an international boundary.
● The **Ichamati River** serves as part of the **international boundary between India and Bangladesh**, making it strategically significant for **border management and security coordination**.
● It is a **transboundary tidal river** shared by **India (West Bengal)** and **Bangladesh**, originating from **Mahjdia village in Nadia district** and flowing about **216 km** before joining the **Kalindi River** at Hasnabad and finally draining into the **Bay of Bengal** near **Moore Island**.
● It is a **bifurcation of the Mathabhanga River** and serves as a **lifeline for thousands of riverine** **communities** in **North 24 Parganas**. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Durga idol immersion on the Ichamati River united people from India and Bangladesh through a centuries-old tradition of shared heritage.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: National Park, Wildlife Sanctuary, Biosphere Reserve
questions.questionText:
Indian Pangolin is found in which of the following
protected areas?
I. 	 Kanha Tiger Reserve
II. 	 Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary
III. 	Male Mahadeshwara Hills Wildlife Sanctuary
IV. 	 Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Kanha Tiger Reserve**, also known as Kanha National Park, is one of India’s most renowned tiger reserves and the largest national park in Madhya Pradesh. The park supports a large variety of mammals, including tiger, leopard, sloth bear, gaur, sambar, and **Indian pangolin**, among others. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Asola-Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary, located in Delhi** is the only protected area in India representing the northeastern outliers of the Aravalli hill ranges. **Indian Pangolin,** Leopard, Indian Boar, and Sambar Deer are confirmed species at Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary. **So, point II is correct.**
● The **Male Mahadeshwara (MM) Hills Wildlife Sanctuary**, located in Karnatak is home to elephants, tigers, Indian bison (gaur), wild dogs (dholes), leopards, foxes, **sambars,** **Indian Pangolin,** spotted deer, wild boar, sloth bear and numerous bird species. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve (STR)** encompasses the **forests of Erode and Sathyamangalam** **Divisions** in Tamil Nadu. Major fauna includes the Tiger, Elephant,  **Indian Pangolin,** Leopard (Panthera pardus), Sloth Bear**,** Gaur, Blackbuck, Spotted Deer, **Wild Boar,** Black-naped Hare, Common Langur, Nilgiri Langur, Striped-neck Mongoose, and Bonnet Macaque. **So, point IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Madras High Court ordered appropriate action against all illegal resorts and tourist lodges functioning within the prohibited zone of Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following is the correct
descending order of countries by the size of their official government/central banks gold reserves?
I. 	 USA
II. 	 India
III. 	China
IV. 	 Germany
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The correct chronological order is - USA > Germany >China > India**
● Central banks across the world hold **gold** as part of their reserve assets to ensure monetary stability and strengthen international financial credibility**.**
● **The United States** holds the largest gold reserves in the world, amounting to **8,133.5 tonnes,** which constitute about 78.7% of its total foreign reserves as of August 2025.
● **China holds 2,302.3 tonnes of gold, ranking seventh globall**y. Gold constitutes about 7% of China’s total reserves. The People’s Bank of China has steadily increased its gold reserves over the past decade.
● **India** holds **880 tonnes** of gold as of August 2025, making it the **ninth-largest gold holder** globally. Gold accounts for around **13.8%** of India’s total reserves. The Reserve Bank of India has gradually increased its gold holdings over the years, reflecting India’s economic growth, cultural affinity with gold, and diversification strategy.
● **Germany i**s ranked second globally, with official gold holdings of **3,350.3 tonnes,** making up 78.6% of its total reserves as of August 2025. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box** **Top states with the largest gold reserves in India:**
1. Bihar primarily in the Jamui district, which holds about 44% of the country's total gold ore resources.
2. Rajasthan is the second largest state with around 125.9 million tonnes of gold reserves, primarily in the Bhukia-Jagpura Gold Belt in Banswara district.
3. Other states: Karnataka-Andhra Pradesh-Uttar Pradesh.
**Relevance:** The Reserve Bank of India has ramped up its gold reserves, adding $19.14 billion worth of precious metal so far this fiscal year.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Biodiversity (IUCN, WWF, CBD, Cartagena, Nagoya, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the World Commission on
Protected Areas (WCPA), consider the following statements:
I. 	 It is a technical commission of the International
Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN).
II. 	 It presents the Kenton R. Miller Award for
impactful innovation in the management of protected areas.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **World Commission on Protected Areas (WCPA)** is a specialized body within the **International** **Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)**. It was established in 1948 as a global network dedicated to the conservation of nature and the sustainable use of natural resources. It is one of the six technical commissions of the IUCN. These commissions provide scientific and technical expertise to IUCN’s work worldwide. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **Kenton R. Miller Award for Innovation in National Parks and Protected Area Sustainability** was established in **2006** and is presented by the IUCN–WCPA. It is **awarded every two years.** The award honours individuals or teams who have introduced **impactful innovations in the management, planning,** **and governance of protected areas.** In 2025, **Sonali Ghosh**, Director of Kaziranga National Park, became the **first Indian recipient**, recognized for her community-based conservation model. **So,** **statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** In 2025, Sonali Ghosh, Director of Kaziranga National Park, became the first Indian recipient of Kenton R. Miller Award for Innovation in National Parks and Protected Area Sustainability.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.questionText:
'Steadfast Noon' is an annual nuclear deterrence
exercise conducted by which one of the following groups?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Steadfast Noon** is the name of an **annual NATO nuclear deterrence exercise** that involves the training of NATO air forces in the handling and deployment of **U.S. nuclear weapons stored in Europe**.
● The Netherlands was the host nation for this year’s Steadfast Noon. Volkel Air Base was the primary base of focus for the exercise.
● Participation in Steadfast Noon rotates among Allies each year.
● Although no live weapons are used during the drills, the exercise plays a crucial role in **testing NATO’s** **nuclear readiness, operational coordination, and deterrence strategy.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** NATO's annual nuclear deterrence exercise, Steadfast Noon 2025 , was hosted by the Netherlands.

questions.subject: History
questions.questionText:
“Mera Hou Chongba Festival” belongs to which
one of the following States?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Mera Hou Chongba festival** is celebrated in **Manipur** on the 15th lunar day of the Mera month of the **Meitei** calendar every year.
● The festival’s historical roots are believed to date back to the time of Nongda Lairen Pakhangba, a legendary early ruler of **Manipur**.
● Manipur titular King leads a ritual march involving tribal village chiefs from the Manipur Royal Palace to the historic Kangla, where traditional rituals, including Mera Thaomei Thanba (lighting of the ceremonial fire) and Mera Yenkhong Tamba (exchange of gifts).
● Mera Hou Chongba is the only festival in Manipur in which all indigenous communities participate, and it has a significant importance in the unity and communal harmony of Manipur. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** In Manipur, the Mera Hou Chongba festival, which symbolises Hill-valley unity, was celebrated recently.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.questionText:
Methamphetamine, sometimes seen in the
news, is known for which one of the following characteristics?

options.a:
It is a naturally occurring alkaloid used in
traditional medicine.
options.b:
It is a vaccine developed for the treatment of
viral encephalitis.
options.c:
It is a biological pesticide used in organic
farming.
options.d:
It is a highly addictive stimulant that affects
the central nervous system.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Methamphetamine is a synthetic stimulant that directly affects the central nervous system** by increasing dopamine levels in the brain, resulting in intense feelings of euphoria, alertness, and energy. Commonly known as "meth," "crystal meth," "ice," or "Tina," it is frequently abused for its stimulating effects but carries severe health risks. Short-term effects include increased heart rate and blood pressure, decreased appetite, hyperactivity, and insomnia, while long-term use can lead to severe dental problems (often called "meth mouth"), memory loss, cognitive decline, paranoia, hallucinations, psychosis, and a **high risk of addiction** and overdose. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, UNODC reported that a record 236 tons of methamphetamine were seized last year by the region’s governments, 94 percent of it in Southeast Asia.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Minerals and Resources (Coal, Iron, Bauxite, Lead, Zinc, Pyrites, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements regarding
Limestone:
**Statement I:** Limestone mine leaseholders in India can sell or utilize limestone for any purpose without regulatory restrictions.
**Statement II:** The Government of India has classified limestone as a major mineral.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the statements given above?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement II does **not** explain  Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is
correct
